testedConfigurations: Ubuntu 20.04 LTS running on a Dell PowerEdge 740 with an Intel(R) Xeon(R) Platinum 8276 CPU (SkyLake) with PAA Ubuntu 20.04 LTS running on a Dell PowerEdge 740 with an Intel(R) Xeon(R) Platinum 8276 CPU (SkyLake)without PAA
certificate.caveat: Interim validation. When installed, initialized and configured as specified in Section 11.1 of the Security Policy. No assurance of the minimum strength of generated SSPs (e.g., keys)
certificate.description: The PQCryptoLib is a library of cryptographic primitives with a C interface offering security against quantum adversaries.

extraColumns.Use / Function: Non-Approved cryptographic algorithm implementing non- security relevant service. This algorithm is meant to be used between two parties as a method to establish “auxiliary shared secret T” which is used for “hybrid” shared secret Z’ as mentioned in [56Cr2, section 2]. The value Z’ can only be used in the extraction step of the Two- Step Key Derivation process described in [56Cr2, section 5]. Internally, this step is implemented as HKDF-extract function as defined by [RFC5869]. Vendor claims no security over input values consumed by the algorithm and output values generated by the algorithm including value T. The algorithm uses Hash_DRBG for generating random byte strings. It also uses SHA3-{256,512} and SHAKE-{128,256} internally. The algorithm is not intended to be used as a security function and is not used internally by the Module. It must be accessed over API functions that are distinct from APIs used to access approved algorithms.

selfTests.details: DRBG Critical Function Tests (Instantiate, Generate and Reseed)
selfTests.name: DRBG

selfTests.details: Integrity check of cryptographic module, using HMAC with SHA2-512 with fixed 256-bit key over various continuous segments of the Module binary image; tag compared against reference value stored in the binary
selfTests.name: HMAC

selfTests.details: Uses Hash_DRBG based on SHA2-256 for 256-bit security strength. Includes instantiate, generate, generate with additional input, reseed and reseed with additional input KATs. Doesn’t include prediction resistance. Performed before the first random data generation. Self-test is performed before being used in the Integrity test.
selfTests.name: DRBG

selfTests.details: ECDH P-256 Key Generation Pairwise Consistency Test
selfTests.name: ECDH Key Generation

selfTests.details: ECDSA P-256 with SHA2-256 signature generation and verification. Uses 32-byte long message.
selfTests.name: ECDSA

selfTests.details: ECDSA P-256 Key Generation Pairwise Consistency Test
selfTests.name: ECDSA Key Generation

selfTests.details: HMAC-SHA2-512 KAT with 16-byte long key over 32-byte long message. Self-test is performed before being used in the Integrity test.
selfTests.name: HMAC

selfTests.details: HMAC-SHA2-512 KAT with 16-byte long key over 32-byte long message.
selfTests.name: HMAC

selfTests.details: ECC Diffie-Hellman shared secret generation with P-256 as per [IG] D.F
selfTests.name: KAS-SSC

selfTests.details: KDF with HMAC-SHA2-256 is used as PRF in feedback mode with an 8-bit counter located after fixed data, an 8-byte long Label string, and a 32-byte long IV set to buffer filled with random data.
selfTests.name: KDF [108]

selfTests.details: HKDF with HMAC-SHA2-256 as an auxiliary function. An input is a 32-byte long value Z, 16- byte long salt and 52-byte long info string as described by the [56Cr2]. Output (DKM) is a 32- byte long derived key.
selfTests.name: KDA HKDF

selfTests.details: Two-step KDF with HMAC-SHA2-384 as an auxiliary function and concatenation of shared Z and auxiliary secret T as described by the [56Cr2]. An input to the extract step is a 32-byte long value Z and a 16-byte value T. Extraction uses a 128-byte long salt filled with 0. It produces a 48-byte-long KDK. An expansion step uses feedback mode with 16-byte long IV and it does not use counter. An input to the expansion step is a KDK and info string. Info string is formatted as a concatenation of two, 16 bytes long buffers, value T, and a 4-byte long buffer containing a length of output in bits. Output (DKM) is a 32-byte long key.
selfTests.name: KDA Two- Step

selfTests.details: SHAKE128 using 16-byte message. Two separated self-tests with the same parameters run for AVX2 optimized and non- optimized implementation.
selfTests.name: SHA3/SHAKE

selfTests.details: Uses random 34-byte long shared secret Z (as defined in the [56Cr2]) and ClientHello, ServerHello, client Finished and server Finished (as defined in the [TLSACVP] and [RFC8446]) – all 34 byte-long. All key schedule secrets are generated and validated against the expected value.
selfTests.name: TLS KDF v1.3

Non-Approved cryptographic algorithm implementing non- security relevant service. This algorithm is meant to be used between two parties as a method to establish “auxiliary shared secret T” which is used for “hybrid” shared secret Z’ as mentioned in [56Cr2, section 2]. The value Z’ can only be used in the extraction step of the Two- Step Key Derivation process described in [56Cr2, section 5]. Internally, this step is implemented as HKDF-extract function as defined by [RFC5869]. Vendor claims no security over input values consumed by the algorithm and output values generated by the algorithm including value T. The algorithm uses Hash_DRBG for generating random byte strings. It also uses SHA3-{256,512} and SHAKE-{128,256} internally. The algorithm is not intended to be used as a security function and is not used internally by the Module. It must be accessed over API functions that are distinct from APIs used to access approved algorithms.

The RDSEED is CPU instruction, that provides an access to the implementation of XOR-NRBG construction (as per [90C]). It uses AES/128-CBC-MAC conditioner (vetted conditioning component as per [90B]) and internal implementation of DRBG based on AES-CTR to produce full entropy output. RDSEED is used as the main entropy source, which provides 256 bits of entropy to seed DRBG. Construction is CAVP certified (Cert. #A1791).

The Module fails a KAT or PCT self-test. The module does not perform any cryptographic functions and all data output is inhibited in the error state. The Module needs to be power cycled to clear the error.
The Module enters the ERROR state and outputs status of PQCL_STATE_ERROR
DRBG Critical Function Tests (Instantiate, Generate and Reseed)

Integrity check of cryptographic module, using HMAC with SHA2-512 with fixed 256-bit key over various continuous segments of the Module binary image; tag compared against reference value stored in the binary

Uses Hash_DRBG based on SHA2-256 for 256-bit security strength. Includes instantiate, generate, generate with additional input, reseed and reseed with additional input KATs. Doesn’t include prediction resistance. Performed before the first random data generation. Self-test is performed before being used in the Integrity test.
ECDH P-256 Key Generation Pairwise Consistency Test
ECDSA P-256 with SHA2-256 signature generation and verification. Uses 32-byte long message.
ECDSA P-256 Key Generation Pairwise Consistency Test
HMAC-SHA2-512 KAT with 16-byte long key over 32-byte long message. Self-test is performed before being used in the Integrity test.
HMAC-SHA2-512 KAT with 16-byte long key over 32-byte long message.
ECC Diffie-Hellman shared secret generation with P-256 as per [IG] D.F
KDF with HMAC-SHA2-256 is used as PRF in feedback mode with an 8-bit counter located after fixed data, an 8-byte long Label string, and a 32-byte long IV set to buffer filled with random data.

HKDF with HMAC-SHA2-256 as an auxiliary function. An input is a 32-byte long value Z, 16- byte long salt and 52-byte long info string as described by the [56Cr2]. Output (DKM) is a 32- byte long derived key.

Two-step KDF with HMAC-SHA2-384 as an auxiliary function and concatenation of shared Z and auxiliary secret T as described by the [56Cr2]. An input to the extract step is a 32-byte long value Z and a 16-byte value T. Extraction uses a 128-byte long salt filled with 0. It produces a 48-byte-long KDK. An expansion step uses feedback mode with 16-byte long IV and it does not use counter. An input to the expansion step is a KDK and info string. Info string is formatted as a concatenation of two, 16 bytes long buffers, value T, and a 4-byte long buffer containing a length of output in bits. Output (DKM) is a 32-byte long key.
SHAKE128 using 16-byte message. Two separated self-tests with the same parameters run for AVX2 optimized and non- optimized implementation.

Uses random 34-byte long shared secret Z (as defined in the [56Cr2]) and ClientHello, ServerHello, client Finished and server Finished (as defined in the [TLSACVP] and [RFC8446]) – all 34 byte-long. All key schedule secrets are generated and validated against the expected value.
